prior.content_md:
*This section documents the ETS obligations that apply to the workspace's carrier ecosystem, filtered by transport mode and trade lane. The workspace does not hold direct compliance obligations; the obligations below sit with carriers. They are documented here because they drive cost pass-through and client-disclosure requirements.*

## Air — Primary Mode | EU ETS Aviation

**Scope:** EU ETS covers domestic aviation within the EEA and, following recent extensions, certain departing/arriving flights. Aircraft operators must surrender EU Allowances (EUAs) for covered emissions.

**Current price:** EUR 74.35 (USD 84.01) average secondary market price in 2025.

**Cap:** 26.2 MtCO2e (2026, aviation).

**Effect on the workspace (air):** Every air carrier operating intra-EEA or on covered routes incurs EUA costs at the current price. These costs are passed to freight customers. For time-sensitive, high-value cargo (live events, fine art, film and TV) where air is non-substitutable, the workspace has no mode-shift lever. The ETS cost is embedded in every EEA air shipment.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — EU ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Air — Japan GX-ETS

**Scope:** Japan GX-ETS covers domestic aviation and transport. In force as of the ICAP data cycle.

**Current allowance price:** Not published in available ICAP data for the GX-ETS entry. Effect on Japan air corridor requires carrier-specific data.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — Japan GX-ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Air — Korea K-ETS

**Scope:** K-ETS covers domestic aviation. In force since 2015.

**Current price:** KRW 9,393 (USD 6.60) secondary market, 2025.

**Effect on the workspace (Korea air):** Modest per-tonne price relative to EU ETS. Pass-through on Korea air routes for luxury goods and automotive cargo is low in absolute terms at current prices but should be tracked as K-ETS cap tightens.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — Korea ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Road — Secondary Mode | Germany National ETS

**Scope:** Germany National ETS covers transport fuel use (road). In force since 2021. Fixed price EUR 55 (USD 62.15) in 2025; transitions to auctioning with price corridor in 2026.

**Total revenue 2025:** EUR 16 billion (USD 18.1 billion) — indicating the scale of cost flowing through the German transport fuel supply chain.

**Effect on the workspace (road in Germany):** Road carriers operating in Germany embed this carbon cost in fuel surcharges or rates. For the workspace's European road movements — particularly high-value automotive and live events requiring dedicated trucking through Germany — this is a current embedded cost.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — German National ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Road — Austria National ETS

**Scope:** Austrian National ETS covers transport fuel use. Fixed price EUR 55 (USD 62) through 2027.

**Effect on the workspace (road in Austria):** Same mechanism as Germany. Pass-through via carrier fuel surcharges on Austria road movements.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — Austrian National ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Road — Québec Cap-and-Trade (Americas)

**Scope:** Québec Cap-and-Trade covers transport fuel. In force since 2013, linked with California.

**Current price:** CAD 48.61 (USD 35.24) weighted average secondary market, 2025.

**Effect on the workspace (road in Québec/Canada):** Relevant for live events or film and TV cargo moving by road in Québec. Pass-through mechanism same as above.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — Canada Québec entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Road — California Cap-and-Trade (Americas)

**Scope:** California Cap-and-Trade covers transport fuel distributors. In force since 2013.

**Current price:** USD 29.10 secondary market, 2025.

**Effect on the workspace (road in California):** Road carriers in California embed this cost. For the workspace's live events, high-value automotive, and luxury goods routed by road in California, this is a current cost embedded in rates.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — USA California entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Road — Washington Cap-and-Invest (Americas)

**Scope:** Washington Cap-and-Invest covers transport and other sectors. In force since 2023.

**Current price:** USD 61.36 secondary market, 2025 — the highest sub-national carbon price in the Americas per ICAP data.

**Effect on the workspace (road in Washington):** Road carriers in Washington State pass through this cost. At USD 61.36/tonne, Washington's carbon price is more than double California's on a per-tonne basis and approaching EU levels, with meaningful impact on trucking rates for workspace cargo moving through the Pacific Northwest.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — USA Washington entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Ocean — Tertiary Mode | EU ETS Maritime

**Scope:** EU ETS was extended to cover maritime from 2024. Vessel operators on qualifying voyages to/from EU ports surrender EUAs. Phase 1 covers 50% of emissions on relevant voyages; Phase 2 increases this to 100%.

**Current price:** EUR 74.35 (USD 84.01) average secondary market, 2025 (same EUA pool as power and industry).

**Cap:** 1,185.4 MtCO2e (2026, covering electricity/heat generation, industrial manufacturing, and maritime transport combined).

**Effect on the workspace (ocean to/from EU):** For high-value automotive or large-format live events equipment moved by ocean to EU ports, carrier EUA costs are being passed through. The effect is proportional to cargo weight and voyage emissions share. Exact per-unit pass-through requires carrier-specific invoice data.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — EU ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Ocean — UK ETS Maritime

**Scope:** UK ETS covers maritime (and domestic aviation, industry, power). In force since 2021.

**Current price:** GBP 50.34 (USD 66.28) secondary market, 2025.

**Cap:** 77.4 MtCO2e (2026).

**Effect on the workspace (ocean via UK ports):** Vessel operators on covered UK voyages incur UK ETS costs. For the workspace's ocean shipments touching UK ports, pass-through applies at GBP 50.34/tonne — close in magnitude to the EU ETS price.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — UK ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Ocean — New Zealand ETS

**Scope:** New Zealand ETS covers maritime, transport, waste, forestry, and other sectors. In force since 2008.

**Current price:** NZD 55.04 (USD 31.99) secondary market, 2025.

**Effect on the workspace (ocean via New Zealand):** Relevant only if the workspace routes ocean cargo via New Zealand. At current volumes for the workspace's described verticals, this is likely a low-frequency exposure; flag for review if New Zealand routes are used for high-value automotive.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — New Zealand ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---
